background: For the illustrative example, we have established that when \bar{x}=173 (pounds), P=0.27. This P-value indicates that 173 is not significantly different from 170 at conventional levels of \alpha (retain H_0). In contrast, when \bar{x}=185, P=0.0013, thus providing highly significant evidence against H_0 (reject H_0).

task.query: When \bar{x}=173, is the P-value significant at conventional levels of \alpha?
task.answer: The P-value of 0.27 indicates that 173 is not significantly different from 170 at conventional levels of \alpha (retain H_0).

task.query: When \bar{x}=185, is there significant evidence against H_0?
task.answer: The P-value of 0.0013 provides highly significant evidence against H_0 (reject H_0).
